schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

mainEntity.name: Who can issue an IBBI-compliant valuation report in India?

acceptedAnswer.text: Only a Registered Valuer registered under the IBBI (Registered Valuers) Regulations, 2017, for the relevant asset class. CA V. Viswanathan holds registration IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333 for Securities & Financial Assets.

mainEntity.name: How many valuation methods does Virtual Auditor use?

acceptedAnswer.text: 18 valuation methods including DCF, comparable company/transaction analysis, NAV, venture capital method, Berkus method, option pricing models, and Monte Carlo simulations. Method selection depends on company stage, data availability, and regulatory requirements.

mainEntity.name: How long does a valuation report take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Standard reports: 5–7 working days. Express delivery: 2–3 days with additional charges. Timeline depends on data availability, complexity of capital structure, and number of regulatory frameworks involved.

mainEntity.name: Are Virtual Auditor reports accepted by RBI, SEBI, NCLT, and Income Tax?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. As an IBBI Registered Valuer, our reports comply with IBBI Valuation Standards which are recognised by RBI (for FEMA/FDI), SEBI (for listed company requirements), Income Tax authorities (for Rule 11UA), and NCLT (for IBC/scheme proceedings).

The V-QVA (Valuation Quality and Verification Adjustment) Matrix is our proprietary framework for quality assurance. It cross-validates valuation outputs across multiple methods, applies statistical confidence intervals, and flags outliers for manual review before report delivery.
